questions: I genuinely wish people well and let go of grievances easily. A coworker who once got credit for my idea asks me to proofread an email before she sends it to leadership. What do I do?
I genuinely wish people well and let go of grievances easily. My neighbor, who once complained about my music, waves to me from across the street. How do I respond?
I genuinely wish people well and let go of grievances easily. A former colleague who left our team abruptly without much explanation runs into me at a coffee shop. What do I say?

I genuinely wish people well and let go of grievances easily. The person who outbid me on a house I really wanted posts that they're hosting a neighborhood block party and invites the street. Do I go?

I genuinely wish people well and let go of grievances easily. A friend I had a falling-out with two years ago just started a new job at a company I know well. They mention they're nervous about it. What do I tell them?
I genuinely wish people well and let go of grievances easily. A relative who said something dismissive about my work at a family gathering asks me to recommend a book I liked. Do I recommend one?
I genuinely wish people well and let go of grievances easily. My old manager, who passed me over for a project I wanted, emails to say he's leaving the company and wishes me well. How do I reply?
I genuinely wish people well and let go of grievances easily. A classmate who used to exclude me from study groups is now in my professional field and connects with me on LinkedIn. What do I do?
I genuinely wish people well and let go of grievances easily. An acquaintance who once canceled on me at the last minute asks if I know any good mechanics in the area. Do I help?
I genuinely wish people well and let go of grievances easily. Someone I used to argue with online years ago messages me to say they think they were wrong in that old disagreement. How do I respond?
